reasoning: The reference value of 1.000 on 2026-01-08 implies that there were exactly zero ACLED "Battles" in the preceding 360 days (since the formula is 1 + 30-day average, where 0 average gives 1.000). To resolve positively, there must be >10 times this baseline (i.e., at least 11 battles) in a 30-day period. For the first resolution date (Jan 25), the 30-day period is already 75% elapsed with no reported armed conflicts or battles, making 11+ battles in the remaining 7 days practically impossible. For the second date (Feb 17), the 30-day window starts from the forecast date. Current news indicates that 2025 has been a year of recovery and lifting of protest bans after the May 2024 riots, and there are currently no known organized armed groups engaging in sustained warfare in New Caledonia. Thus, 11+ battles in 30 days is extremely unlikely. Following the system rules to keep probabilities at 0.05 or higher and increase uncertainty for further horizons, I estimate 5% and 6%.
